# Glossary

<!-- awf:edit terms ‚Äî from .awf/docs/parts/glossary/terms.md -->
Project jargon and what each term means; start here when a term is unfamiliar. Sorted by term.

| Term | Meaning |
|---|---|
| anchored glob | awf's single glob dialect (ADR-0077, `internal/pathglob`): a pattern matches a file's full slash-separated repo-relative path ‚Äî `*.go` is top-level only, `**/*.go` is any depth, `cmd/**` a subtree. No basename mode; deliberately stricter than gitignore's floating slash-free patterns. |
| claimed-path model | The ADR-0086 allowlist deciding what may live under `.awf/`: the skeleton (`config.yaml`, `awf.lock`), the enabled render units, enabled artifacts' sidecars and declared-section parts, and the singleton sidecars/parts, with `memory/**` exempt. Derived from config + catalog + the RenderAll output; every entry outside it is failing `awf check` drift, collapsed to the topmost unclaimed directory. |
| checker-cmd idiom | The shape shared by the repo-only gate/release checkers (`cmd/covercheck`, `cmd/deadcodecheck`, `cmd/releasecheck`, `cmd/pincheck`, `cmd/mutants`): a coverage-ignored `main` that only `os.Exit`s a unit-tested `run(‚Ä¶, stdout, stderr) int` seam, so the logic meets the 100% floor while the wrapper stays one line. |
| domain paths | A domain's file territory: anchored globs under `paths:` in the domain sidecar `.awf/domains/<name>.yaml`. Input to the `domain-code-staleness` audit rule ‚Äî territory churn without a current-state co-change warns; a domain without `paths` opts out (ADR-0077). |
| dormant doc-gated skill | A skill enabled while its required doc is disabled ‚Äî the pre-schema-8 valid state that silently rendered nothing (ADR-0013 suppression). The `close-enabled-set` migration drops it (preserving observed output); post-ADR-0081 the state is refused at open. |
| plan op | A single enable-array change in an `awf add`/`awf remove` resolver plan (ADR-0081): one artifact node, an add/remove direction, and required-by provenance. Printed one per line before any config change; a whole plan lands in one rewrite. |
| promotion ladder | `awf-retrospective`'s routing rule for a recurring, codifiable observation: promote it to the strongest rung it can support ‚Äî an invariant (via ADR, `inv:` slug plus backing comment), a gate test or lint rule, a code-review focus item, or a pitfalls note (ADR-0067). |
| prose knob | A catalog var descriptor whose only effect is tuning the wording of rendered prose ‚Äî the category ADR-0084 bans. Contrast a functional value: a command, an enforced identifier set, or a structural path that the rendered artifacts or the tooling execute or enforce. The value-carrying descriptor set is pinned by a test; prose customization goes through convention parts instead. |
| resync | The plan‚ÜîADR reconciliation pass (`awf-reviewing-plan-resync`) that runs before implementation when both a plan and at least one ADR exist; it re-runs the scope-completeness and doc-currency lenses to catch plan-vs-finalised-ADR(s) drift and loops until they converge. |
| retrospective | The terminal workflow-chain step (`awf-retrospective`), run by the main thread after `awf-reviewing-impl`: it reflects on the session, records worthy observations, and promotes recurring findings up the promotion ladder toward deterministic checks (ADR-0067). |
| stub | Must-replace starter content: a section default declared with the `stub` marker attribute, or a convention part carrying the whole-line `<!-- awf:stub -->` marker. Rendered publication-safe but reported by the `awf check`/`awf init` unauthored-content advisory until authored (ADR-0070). |
| sync provenance line | The per-file cause `awf sync` prints when a rendered file's output moves against the prior lock: `template` (upstream template source), `config` (the project's own vars/sidecars/parts), `template+config`, `internal` (a non-hashed input such as the pinned binary version), `regenerated` (a generated index, which carries no hashes), or `added` (no prior entry) ‚Äî attributed from the lock's template/config/output hashes; first syncs and byte-identical re-renders print nothing. Distinct from the other two "provenance" senses: `awf:edit` pointer comments and plan-op required-by lines. |
| template source residue | awf-specific content surviving in shipped template sources ‚Äî a concrete ADR citation (`ADR-` + four digits; the `ADR-NNNN` placeholder is legal) or a repo-identity literal outside the pinned three-entry exemption list. Banned by a source-level scan over the embedded templates FS, all conditional branches included (ADR-0082; list extended by ADR-0085). |
| trust-bearing file | A file whose content other awf decisions trust without re-derivation: `.awf/awf.lock` (the drift and schema oracle) and the user-authored `.awf/config.yaml` when a migration rewrites it. Written atomically via `manifest.WriteFileAtomic`; a present-but-unreadable lock is a hard error in every reader (ADR-0076). |
| upgrade porcelain | The rendered `.awf/upgrade.sh`, the deliberately nondeterministic layer over the deterministic `.awf/bootstrap.sh` (git's porcelain/plumbing split): it resolves a target version ‚Äî `$1` or the newest release ‚Äî fetches through the bootstrap via the `AWF_VERSION` override, and execs `awf upgrade` as its final statement (ADR-0085). |
| working memory | The per-effort session-state file `.awf/memory/<effort-slug>.md` ‚Äî evolving design brief, chain position (`Phase:`/`Next:` lines), handoff log ‚Äî checkpointed by the chain skills and kept out of version control by the always-rendered self-ignoring `.awf/memory/.gitignore`; never committed or cited by an ADR, plan, or commit message, and deleted by the retrospective when the chain terminates (ADR-0069; read on demand rather than as a fixed startup step, ADR-0075). |
